content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN. Your task is to generate a new binary string TTT of the same length such that each corresponding character of TTT is different from the character at the same position in SSS.

In other words, for each position iii (0≤i<N0 ≤ i < N0≤i<N), the following condition must hold Ti≠SiT_i ≠ S_iTi​=Si​.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN — the length of SSS.
The next line contains the binary string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a binary string TTT of length NNN, where Ti≠SiT_i ≠ S_iTi​=Si​ for all valid indices iii.
constraints: SSS is a binary string, i.e, contains only the characters 000 and 111.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 444: T→[1100]T \xrightarrow{} [1100]T​[1100], since T0≠S0T_0 
eq S_0T0​=S0​, T1≠S1T_1 
eq S_1T1​=S1​, T2≠S2T_2 
eq S_2T2​=S2​ and T3≠S3T_3 
eq S_3T3​=S3​, TTT is a valid output.
